pattern: Fixing the middle pair where the earlier index holds the larger value turns counting these zig-zag quadruplets into multiplying an easy-to-track count of qualifying left partners by a count of qualifying right partners.
hints: The middle two positions must form a local inversion (the earlier one bigger than the later one), since the pattern needs the later-middle value to be the smaller of the two.

For a fixed such middle pair, any earlier position with a smaller value than the second middle value, combined with any later position with a larger value than the first middle value, completes a valid quadruplet.

Precompute, for each starting position, how many earlier values are below a given threshold, and for each ending position, how many later values are above a given threshold, so each middle pair's contribution is a single multiplication.

edge_inputs.description: a small permutation with one qualifying quadruplet
a small permutation with no qualifying quadruplet
the minimum length, strictly increasing (no valid inversion exists)
a zig-zag permutation with several overlapping candidate quadruplets
a permutation with the pattern repeated at different scales
a larger permutation near the oracle-checkable size

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 1000))
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]

stress.description: a random permutation near the maximum allowed size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 4000))
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
